behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the model’s content selection quality: whether it grounds the reply in thread-specific details. A unit/integration test with canned outputs cannot expose generic-but-fluent real model behavior. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model decision (draft generation), not multiple agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real drafting model on a set of real/realistic email threads with required anchor points (e.g., dates, commitments, questions). Assert the draft includes thread-specific references to those anchors (semantic match/rubric), and fails when output is generic. Execute multiple runs per thread to capture nondeterministic misses.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires real contradiction judgment against KB content; that correctness depends on model output quality if the checker is LLM/ML-based. Mocked checker responses would hide real false negatives/positives. Rejected Workflow: this behavior isolates the checker decision itself, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Provide drafts containing known contradictory and non-contradictory claims against a real KB slice, run the real consistency-check component, and assert contradictory claims are flagged while supported claims are not. Repeat runs to measure variance and catch unstable contradiction detection.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is hallucination vs disclaimer policy adherence, which depends on real model generation behavior under empty/irrelevant KB context. Stubbed outputs would always pass and miss fabrication failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior can be validated at the single generation decision conditioned on 'no relevant entries'.
behaviors.test_description: Run real draft generation with threads whose required facts are absent from the KB (retrieval returns none). Assert output contains the required disclaimer language/policy and does not introduce unsupported concrete facts. Use repeated runs and adversarial prompts to catch occasional fabrication.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff/state-gating boundary (draft state, approval state, send service trigger). This is not model-quality dependent. Integration-brake checks: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required to prove gate logic, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text is about approval gating, which can be caught by wiring workflow modules with a fake sender and asserting no send call pre-approval.
behaviors.test_description: Wire workflow service + persistence + send adapter in-process. Create draft, advance workflow without approval, assert send adapter not invoked and state remains pending. Then record explicit approval event and assert exactly one send invocation occurs afterward.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly spans multiple sequential agentic stages (generation + factual checking) with orchestration across steps. Lower levels can validate components but miss cross-step degradation (e.g., generation ambiguity causing checker miss, or checker output not propagated to approval view). Rejected Agentic: behavior text requires the full sequence, not one model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real email thread artifact: ingest thread, run real draft generation, run real fact check against KB, and verify approval UI/state receives the resulting draft plus flags. Assert stage-to-stage artifacts are preserved and coherent across the full run.
